nodes.label: AI as idea generator and organizer
nodes.label_negative: AI as mechanical and lacking human understanding
nodes.grounding_span: AI is not used exclusively by either of my jobs how ever it is something I use to generate ideas and support both the teachers and parents

nodes.label: AI as emotional support in difficult situations
nodes.label_negative: AI as unable to grasp emotional experiences
nodes.grounding_span: AI helps to 1. help me decompress if it was a rather difficult interaction

nodes.label: AI as planning and structuring tool
nodes.label_negative: AI as ignoring real-world time and human factors
nodes.grounding_span: AI does not have a good grasp on the amount of time it takes for certain things

nodes.label: AI as skill development enabler
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement that diminishes human skills
nodes.grounding_span: AI has helped me sharpen my skills

nodes.label: Human touch in language teaching for engagement
nodes.grounding_span: I want to work very hard not to use AI differently

nodes.label: Professional confidence and validation
nodes.grounding_span: It feels good to be able to be confident in directing the parents on what is next

nodes.label: Authenticity and personal integrity
nodes.grounding_span: I would never want to use AI so much that I simply become an assembly line for my clients thoughts and issues

nodes.label: Workflow efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI does the work of sorting and my brain has more space to be creative

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for organizing brain dumps
nodes.grounding_span: Working with AI to figure out parenting plans has been helpful because I am able to enter in the brain dump of the notes I took while in the session with the parent and then it organizes it all

nodes.label: Frustration with AI's mechanical understanding of time and emotion
nodes.grounding_span: Mostly just that sometimes AI does not understand those human experiences of time and emotion

nodes.label: Resistance to over-reliance on AI
nodes.grounding_span: I want to work very hard not to use AI differently

nodes.label: Present situation and adapt — uses AI as springboard, ignores follow-ups
nodes.grounding_span: I typically present the situation and let it generate suggestions that I then adapt. In fact I find myself often ignoring the follow up questions

nodes.label: Independent adoption and pioneering use
nodes.grounding_span: I am one of the only ones who use AI on a regular basis and only because I am acquainted with it from outside my normal working hours
